nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: 白眉弯嘴鹛
nodes.summary: 白眉弯嘴鹛（White-browed babbler，学名 Pomatostomus superciliosus），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，常见栖息地包括灌丛、稀树草原。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: The habitat for the white-browed babbler varies across the southern regions of Australia, from dry sclerophyll woodlands, shrublands, heathland, semiarid grasslands and open forests. They prefer areas with a dense understory of shrubs or spinifex for protection and nesting. Their range extends through arid and semiarid areas all the way across the South Australian coastline to Western Australia, and by doing so, it crosses many different ecological vegetation classes (EVCs). No preference seems to be shown with the overall composition of habitat, only that when the understory provides good quality protection from the environment and predators, the species is more successful. Populations have been found in many locations across southern Australia, including dry, rocky gibber desert, mulga, eucalyptusor acacia woodlands Studies have also found many populations in and around human-related infrastructure (see below). Babblers spend the majority of their time foraging through leaf litter on the ground, although during nesting periods they retreat to nests inside hollowed logs, grass clumps, fallen branches or shrubs
